case.title: JOHNSON v. STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA

opinion.h1: JOHNSON v. STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA, 396 U.S. 22 (1969)
opinion.h4: JOHNSON v. STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT No. 434. Decided November 10, 1969
opinion.p: 268 Cal. App. 2d 437, 74 Cal. Rptr. 11, appeal dismissed and certiorari denied.
The appeal is dismissed for want of jurisdiction. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for a writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h4: TRANSPORTATION UNLIMITED OF CALIFORNIA v. UNITED STATES, 396 U.S. 22 (1969)
opinion.p: 396 U.S. 22 TRANSPORTATION UNLIMITED OF CALIFORNIA, INC. v. UNITED STATES ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE CENTRAL DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA No. 451. Decided November 10, 1969

Solicitor General Griswold, Assistant Attorney General McLaren, Robert W. Ginnane, and Raymond M. Zimmet for the United States et al., Francis W. McInerny for Midwest Coast Transport, Inc., et al., and David Axelrod for Little Audrey's Transportation Co., Inc., et al., appellees.
The motions to affirm are granted and the judgment is affirmed.
